Block.Para: An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (expenses incurred by caregivers)

This bill was introduced during the First Session of the 40th Parliament. Pursuant to the Standing Orders of the House of Commons, it is deemed to have been considered and approved at all stages completed at the time of prorogation of the First Session. The number of the bill remains unchanged.

Also available on the Parliament of Canada Web Site at the following address:
http://www.parl.gc.ca

This enactment amends the Income Tax Act to allow a taxpayer who provides care to a member of the taxpayer芒聙聶s family 芒聙聰 芒聙聹member of the taxpayer's family芒聙聺 being defined in a broad sense 芒聙聰 to deduct the cost of reasonably necessary goods, equipment and services purchased or leased in order to care for the individual if the individual is entitled to an impairment credit under section 118.3 of that Act.
A taxpayer may be a caregiver by voluntary act. It is not necessary that the nearest relative be the caregiver.
Expenses that would be incurred if the member of the taxpayer's family were not impaired, such as for food, normal household supplies and normal utilities, are excluded.
An Act to amend the Income Tax Act (expenses incurred by caregivers)
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:
1. The Income Tax Act is amended by adding the following after section 118.4:

Para.change: 118.41 (1) The definitions in this subsection apply in this section.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹caregiver expense芒聙聺
脗芦 d脙漏pense de l芒聙聶aidant naturel 脗禄

Para.change: 芒聙聹caregiver expense芒聙聺 means the cost of purchasing or leasing any goods, equipment or services prescribed by the regulations that are reasonably necessary to enable the taxpayer to care for an individual mentioned in subsection (2), including special equipment, services or modifications brought to a dwelling in order to feed, protect and care for the individual or to improve the individual芒聙聶s mobility or general well-being, but does not include the cost of food, household supplies, normal utilities and other household expenses that would normally have been incurred by the individual if the individual were not impaired.

Block.MarginalNote:
芒聙聹member of the taxpayer芒聙聶s family芒聙聺
脗芦 membre de la famille du contribuable 脗禄

Para.change: 芒聙聹member of the taxpayer芒聙聶s family芒聙聺 includes
(a) the taxpayer's spouse or common-law partner; and
(b) the taxpayer's child, grandchild, parent, grandparent, sibling, aunt or uncle, or a person in an equivalent relationship with the taxpayer as a result of

(2) There may be deducted from the taxable income of a taxpayer for a taxation year the aggregate of all amounts each of which is an amount paid in the year by the taxpayer for caregiver expenses necessary to care for an individual who was, in the year,
(a) a member of the taxpayer芒聙聶s family;
(b) entitled to a credit under section 118.3 in respect of an impairment; and
(c) as a result of a normal family obligation or the voluntary undertaking by the taxpayer to be a caregiver, dependent on the taxpayer for care by reason of the impairment.

Block.Para:
